channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Political commentator on the recent SCOTUS rulings and their effect on Trump's agenda
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Jonah Goldberg of The Dispatch about how this term's Supreme Court decisions are reflected in President Trump's approach to his signature issues and executive power.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Jonah Goldberg of The Dispatch about how this term's Supreme Court decisions are reflected in President Trump's approach to his signature issues and executive power.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: The majority of Americans are proud but worry about direction of the country
item.description: As the U.S. approaches its 250th birthday, Americans reflect on the state of the nation in the latest NPR/PBS News/Marist poll.
item.content:encoded: As the U.S. approaches its 250th birthday, Americans reflect on the state of the nation in the latest NPR/PBS News/Marist poll.
item.dc:creator: Saige Miller

item.title: Supreme Court deals some blows to Trump's agenda but leaves him with more expansive powers
item.description: President Trump has trumpeted his victories and sought workarounds for his losses.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump has trumpeted his victories and sought workarounds for his losses.

(Image credit: Alex Wong)
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith

item.title: He sent a harsh email to ICE's top official. 5 months later, federal agents tracked him down
item.description: Federal agents went to a Rochester man's home to warn him that an email he sent five months ago could be an illegal threat. He was on vacation but another agent found his hotel hundreds of miles away.
item.content:encoded:
Federal agents went to a Rochester man's home to warn him that an email he sent five months ago could be an illegal threat. He was on vacation but another agent found his hotel hundreds of miles away.

(Image credit: David Streever)
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block

item.title: World Cup's knockout rounds collide with a massive heat wave
item.description: A major heat wave is affecting much of the eastern half of the United States this week, bringing dangerous conditions to multiple World Cup matches.
item.content:encoded:
A major heat wave is affecting much of the eastern half of the United States this week, bringing dangerous conditions to multiple World Cup matches.

(Image credit: Kevin C. Cox)
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Hersher

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: SCOTUS upholds birthright citizenship, Melat Kiros poised to become the first Gen Z woman elected to Congress after Colorado primary, some World Cup matches will contend with major U.S. heat wave.
item.content:encoded: SCOTUS upholds birthright citizenship, Melat Kiros poised to become the first Gen Z woman elected to Congress after Colorado primary, some World Cup matches will contend with major U.S. heat wave.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: SCOTUS upholds bans on transgender athletes participating in women and girls' sports
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday said that states can bar trans girls and women from participating in school sports teams. But states that choose to may also allow trans athletes to participate.
item.content:encoded: The Supreme Court on Tuesday said that states can bar trans girls and women from participating in school sports teams. But states that choose to may also allow trans athletes to participate.
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: Television showrunner Mara Brock Akil talks about her debut novel
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with acclaimed television showrunner and writer Mara Brock Akil about her debut novel, "The Revelation of Dionne Daphne."
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with acclaimed television showrunner and writer Mara Brock Akil about her debut novel, "The Revelation of Dionne Daphne."
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Chris Mosier talks about Supreme Court's decision on transgender athletes
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Chris Mosier, the first openly transgender man to represent the U.S. in international competition, about the Supreme Court's ruling on transgender athletes.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Chris Mosier, the first openly transgender man to represent the U.S. in international competition, about the Supreme Court's ruling on transgender athletes.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Supreme Court ends term with decision to uphold birthright citizenship
item.description: On its final day of its term, the Supreme Court rejected President Trump's executive order that tried to limit birthright citizenship but sided with the White House in many other disputes.
item.content:encoded: On its final day of its term, the Supreme Court rejected President Trump's executive order that tried to limit birthright citizenship but sided with the White House in many other disputes.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Johnson
